replication_study.hypothesis: In the replication dataset, the latent openness to experiences factor (OPEN), measured by parcels Open_Par1–Open_Par3, will positively predict the latent leadership-shaping experiences factor (LEAD), measured by parcels Lead_Par1–Lead_Par3, yielding a positive standardized path coefficient (OPEN → LEAD). As a robustness check, the total openness score (Oppenness_Total) will be positively correlated with the total leadership experiences score (Leader_Exp_Total).

data_plan.source_type: Self-report questionnaire dataset (parcel indicators and scale totals prepared for CFA/SEM and correlations).
data_plan.sample_size: 215 participants (both CFA/SEM and correlations CSVs have 215 rows).
data_plan.access_details: Provided in the replication_data folder of this study package. Files to be mounted/available at /app/data at execution time.

qualification.explanation: The dataset is qualified because it measures the same constructs (adult attachment, trait anxiety, openness to experiences, and leadership-shaping experiences) with parcel indicators aligned to the original study’s SEM specification, enabling a like-for-like test of the focal OPEN → LEAD path in an independent sample. This tests robustness of the original finding on a new dataset collected under similar conceptual conditions.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original study constructs and modeling: The original paper used latent variables for secure attachment (low anxiety and avoidance), trait anxiety (STAI), openness to experiences (abbreviated OE), and leadership-shaping experiences (LSE), analyzed via SEM with parceling (original_paper.pdf, Sections 3.2–3.3; measurement/SEM described on pp. 754–756; Model 1 depicted in Fig. 2 with OPEN → LEAD). The replication dataset provides parcel columns matching the measurement model: AvoidC_Par1, AvoidD_Par2, AttachX_Par3; STAI_Par1–STAI_Par3; Open_Par1–Open_Par3; Lead_Par1–Lead_Par3 (data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv: dataset info shows exactly these columns). It also includes total scores Secure Attachment, STAI_Total, Oppenness_Total, Leader_Exp_Total for correlation checks (Popper Data for Correlations.csv). The original focal effect (OPEN → LEAD β = 0.34, p < .01) is documented in post_registration.json (results.numerical_results) and original_paper.pdf (Section 4.2).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Sample and context differ: The original study surveyed 286 male IDF soldiers during basic training, analyzed SEM on N≈236–238 due to missingness (post_registration.json: data.sample_size; original_paper.pdf Section 3.1). The replication dataset has N=215 participants (both replication CSVs: 215 rows), and includes columns such as Age, Education, and Military_Tenure (Popper Data for Correlations.csv), suggesting a different and more recent sample context; variable labels are in English, whereas the original administered Hebrew versions (original_paper.pdf Sec. 3.2). Thus, the replication uses a new sample with similar constructs but different participants, providing an independent test. Additionally, the replication package is dated 2022 (Final R Code for Popper & Amit Replication_2022.pdf), indicating a later data collection/processing window than the 2009 original.

data_plan.notes: The correlations CSV includes many 'Unnamed' columns that are entirely NaN and can be ignored. The openness total is labeled 'Oppenness_Total' (double 'n' in the file); code accounts for this via case-insensitive matching. Scale item-level data for alpha estimation are not present; only parcels and totals are available. No explicit demographic or sampling frame documentation was found in the replication files beyond the variable names.

steps: Load parcels dataset (Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv) from /app/data.
Fit the four-factor CFA (ATTACH, ANXIETY, OPEN, LEAD) to confirm the measurement model on parcels.
Fit the hypothesized structural Model 1 (ATTACH → ANXIETY, ATTACH → OPEN; LEAD ← ANXIETY + OPEN) using SEM; extract standardized path coefficient for OPEN → LEAD and model fit indices.
Optionally fit alternative models (2–6) for completeness and sensitivity (as in the original and replication R scripts).
Load correlations dataset (Popper Data for Correlations.csv) and compute Pearson correlation between Oppenness_Total and Leader_Exp_Total as a simple descriptive check on the focal relationship.
Save outputs (SEM parameter estimates and fit indices, correlation results, and correlation matrix) to /app/data.
planned_method.models: Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) with CFA for the measurement model, followed by structural Model 1 (primary). Also compute Pearson correlation between openness total and leadership experiences total as a non-latent robustness check.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Leadership-shaping experiences (LEAD), measured as a latent variable via parcels Lead_Par1, Lead_Par2, Lead_Par3; robustness check uses Leader_Exp_Total.
planned_method.independent_variables: Openness to experiences (OPEN), measured as a latent variable via parcels Open_Par1, Open_Par2, Open_Par3; ATTACH and ANXIETY are also included per the structural model.
planned_method.control_variables: Within the SEM framework, ATTACH and ANXIETY are modeled per Model 1: LEAD is regressed on ANXIETY and OPEN; ATTACH predicts ANXIETY and OPEN. No additional demographic controls are modeled, matching the focal original test.
planned_method.tools_software: Python (semopy for SEM/CFA; pandas, numpy, scipy for data handling and correlations).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Standardized path coefficient from OPEN to LEAD in SEM Model 1; Pearson correlation r between Oppenness_Total and Leader_Exp_Total.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Wald z-test for SEM path significance (as provided by semopy) with two-sided alpha=0.05; two-sided t-test for Pearson correlation significance.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion within SEM model fitting (default in semopy for rows with missing parcel values). For correlations, pairwise deletion on the two totals.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single focal path and one supplementary correlation; no multiple-testing correction planned. If additional paths are inspected, treat them as exploratory.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary success criterion: Positive standardized OPEN → LEAD coefficient with p < 0.05. Secondary: Pearson r (Oppenness_Total, Leader_Exp_Total) > 0 and significant at p < 0.05. Consider effect size proximity to the original β≈0.34 and acceptable model fit (e.g., CFI/TLI ≥ 0.90–0.95, RMSEA ≤ 0.08) as supportive evidence.

files./app/data/entrypoint.py: Python translation of the provided R replication script. It reads the CFA/SEM parcels dataset and (optionally) the correlations dataset from /app/data using glob patterns, fits the 4-factor CFA and One-Factor CFA, then fits structural models 1–6 via semopy. It extracts standardized estimates, including the focal OPEN → LEAD path, computes fit indices, and saves results to /app/data/sem_results.json. It also computes a Pearson correlation between Oppenness_Total and Leader_Exp_Total (if available) and writes /app/data/cor_results.json and /app/data/cor_matrix.csv. All I/O strictly uses /app/data. Invoke with: python 'data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication R Script File__py.py'.

codebase.notes: The original R script (replication_data/Popper Replication R Script File.R) is preserved but not executed here per policy. The Python rewrite uses semopy, which may yield slightly different fit indices/standard errors than AMOS/lavaan but should preserve model logic. Ensure semopy and scipy are installed. The script is robust to minor filename variations by using glob patterns in /app/data.

instructions: Ensure the replication CSV files are available in /app/data with names similar to: 'Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv' and 'Popper Data for Correlations.csv' (the script uses flexible glob patterns).
Install dependencies (inside container): pip install numpy pandas scipy semopy scikit-learn networkx
Run the analysis script: python 'data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication R Script File__py.py'

Outputs will be written to /app/data: sem_results.json (SEM parameters and fit), cor_results.json (if totals data present), and cor_matrix.csv (numeric correlation matrix). Review the standardized coefficient for the OPEN → LEAD path in Model 1 and its p-value.
comparison_metrics: Primary: Standardized path coefficient (OPEN → LEAD) and its significance; compare sign and magnitude to original β = 0.34 (p < .01) reported in original_paper.pdf (Section 4.2) and post_registration.json.
Model fit indices: CFI/TLI (NNFI), RMSEA; assess whether replication SEM achieves acceptable fit similar to or within acceptable thresholds relative to the original.
Supplementary: Pearson correlation between Oppenness_Total and Leader_Exp_Total (sign and significance) as a non-latent corroboration.
